examples.why: 'l'une des plusieurs méthodes' is awkward; 'l'une de plusieurs méthodes' or 'l'une des méthodes' reads natively.

editor_note: No semantic drift or hallucination: the qubit counts, dates, the Osprey "nearly quadruple the size of Eagle at 433 qubits" claim, and the CRN caveat ("if an instance is not specified, all backends and jobs across all instances are available") all match the English exactly. The defects are word-choice/register only. In the Falcon bullet, English "valuable platform" appears twice and is rendered two different ways in the same sentence — first as "פלטפורמה ערכית", which is a mistranslation ("ערכי" means ethical/value-laden, not valuable), then correctly as "פלטפורמה יקרת ערך". Separately, "within the same wiring footprint" is calqued as "בתוך אותו טביעת רגל של חיווט" — both a literal calque no Hebrew hardware writer would use and a gender agreement error (טביעת רגל is feminine → "אותה"). "Deprecated" as "מוצאת משימוש" is understandable but stiff; "מיושנת" / "הוצאה משימוש" is the idiomatic register.

editor_note: No semantic drift, hallucination or dropped caveat anywhere — the noise-management overview paragraph, the Admonition about option incompatibility, and the three "Next steps" bullets all track the English faithfully, and register is correctly informal (ראה / למד עוד / חקור / החלט). The defects are cosmetic term-rendering choices: the two technique names are half-translated hybrids — "dynamical decoupling" → "decoupling דינמי" and "Pauli twirling" → "twirling של Pauli" — where Hebrew quantum writing keeps these multiword terms wholly in English (as this same file already does for Sampler, Circuit, and the untouched code comments); the Hebrew adjective grafted onto an English noun reads as neither. Separately, "job" is rendered as "העבודה שלך" (line 32 "שהעבודה שלך תסתיים בזמן סביר" and line 84 "להריץ את העבודה שלך"), a calque that reads as "your work/labour" rather than a submitted compute job; "המשימה" or the kept "job" is the conventional rendering. Two factual/quantity drifts, one in the central claim of a section. (1) In the "5k challenge" section, "5,000 quantum gates in a single computation" is rendered "5,000개의 양자 회로" (5,000 quantum CIRCUITS), swapping the unit that names the whole section — a business learner would take away a false milestone (5,000 circuits vs 5,000 gates), especially since the prior sentence stresses depth/gate count over qubit count. (2) "IBM doubled the quantum volume twice in just one year" (two doublings, ~4x) is flattened to "양자 볼륨을 2배로 늘렸습니다" (doubled once), understating the claim. Secondary MINOR items: "fault-tolerant" is rendered "오류 내성" throughout (lines 52/136/144/166) but "오류에 대한 허용도가 없습니다" at line 54 — an inconsistency; and "near-term" uses the unusual coinage "근기" (근기 양자 컴퓨터) where 단기/근시일 would read more naturally. The two quantity/unit swaps drive the FAIL; the rest of the file (Quantum Volume / NYC-tour analogy, Gross code 288 qubits→12 logical qubits, Starling 200 logical qubits / 100M gates) is faithful and reads naturally.

editor_note: Section 4 inverts the defining property of sparse vs. broad wave functions: English "non-negligible amplitudes" is rendered twice as "nieznaczące amplitudy" (= negligible/insignificant amplitudes), the exact opposite. Line 317 says SQD works well "gdzie niewielka część stanów bazowych ma nieznaczące amplitudy" — the source means a small fraction has NON-negligible (significant) amplitudes; line 319 says a broad wave function is one where "wiele stanów bazowych ma nieznaczące amplitudy," when the source means many have non-negligible amplitudes. This teaches the false idea that a concentrated/sparse state is one where most states are negligible, and makes the definition of "broad" self-contradictory — a core pedagogical concept in this section, so FAIL. Correct rendering would be "niepomijalne" / "znaczące amplitudy." Minor secondary issues: the calqued malformations "nierwariacyjne" (lines 272, 276) and "wariacjonalnego" (line 398) for "niewariacyjne"/"wariacyjnego"; and "support" rendered as "zawierać" at line 37 but consistently as "nośnik" everywhere else.

examples.translation: Podejście SQD działa dobrze dla rzadkich lub skoncentrowanych wave functions, w których jedynie niewielka część stanów bazowych ma nieznaczące amplitudy.
examples.suggested: ...w których jedynie niewielka część stanów bazowych ma niepomijalne (znaczące) amplitudy.
examples.why: 'non-negligible' is inverted to 'nieznaczące' (negligible), reversing the meaning of a sparse/concentrated wave function — the learner is taught the opposite definition.

examples.source: If the wave function is broad (that is, many basis states have non-negligible amplitudes)...
examples.translation: Jeśli wave function jest rozległa (tzn. wiele stanów bazowych ma nieznaczące amplitudy)...
examples.suggested: Jeśli wave function jest rozległa (tzn. wiele stanów bazowych ma niepomijalne/znaczące amplitudy)...
examples.why: Same inversion; 'broad' is defined as 'many states have negligible amplitudes,' which is self-contradictory and false — broad means many states have significant amplitudes.

examples.why: 'entrelaçamento' is a recognizable and internally consistent choice, but Brazilian-PT quantum-computing convention favors 'emaranhamento'. Defensible and consistent, so not a defect — noted only for terminology awareness.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout: no semantic drift, all quantities preserved (20.000–200.000 măsurători, 600/1500/6000/16000 eșantioane per lot, energii), "right particle sector" correctly rendered "sectorul corect de particule", and the four workflow steps translate accurately. Register is consistently informal "tu" (Construiește, rulează, Reține). Three minor terminology slips a native technical editor would fix: (1) "closed-shell Hartree Fock" is rendered "Hartree Fock cu cochilie închisă" — "cochilie" means a seashell/conch; the conventional Romanian chemistry term is "strat/înveliș închis" (the concept is still recoverable in context). (2) "single ancilla qubit" is calqued as "un singur qubit ansilă"; standard would be "qubit auxiliar/ancilla". (3) "lone pair orbitals" flattened to "perechile singulare" (dropping "orbitali", and "singulare" for "lone" is an imperfect choice). None of these teach a false concept, so none rises to FAIL.

examples.source: The reference orbitals are calculated with closed-shell Hartree Fock using the cc-pvdz basis set.
examples.translation: Orbitalii de referință sunt calculați cu Hartree Fock cu cochilie închisă, folosind setul de baze cc-pvdz.
examples.suggested: ...cu Hartree Fock cu strat închis (closed-shell)...
examples.why: 'cochilie' denotes a seashell/conch; the conventional Romanian term for a closed electronic shell is 'strat/înveliș închis'. Recognizable but non-standard.

examples.source: ...when these qubits are neighbors through a single ancilla qubit.
examples.translation: ...atunci când acești qubiți sunt vecini printr-un singur qubit ansilă.
examples.suggested: ...printr-un singur qubit auxiliar (ancilla).
examples.why: 'ansilă' is a raw calque of 'ancilla' and not an established Romanian term; 'auxiliar' (or kept English 'ancilla') is the conventional rendering.

examples.source: we only include the sigma, sigma*, and lone pair orbitals in the active space
examples.translation: includem doar orbitalii sigma, sigma* și perechile singulare în spațiul activ
examples.suggested: ...și orbitalii perechilor de electroni neparticipanți (lone pair)...
examples.why: 'perechile singulare' drops 'orbitali' and renders 'lone pair' imprecisely; the standard concept is 'pereche de electroni neparticipanți/neîmperecheați'.

editor_note: Faithful and pedagogically clear throughout; meaning is preserved everywhere (surface codes encode one qubit not two, rough/smooth edge mapping, X-left/right vs Z-top/bottom chains, [[19,1,5]] and gross [[144,12,12]] weight-6 details all correct), informal "tu" register consistent (decupezi, întinzi, Amintește-ți). The only defect is a grammatical agreement slip in the three-coloring definition on line 76: "nicio două fețe de aceeași culoare" mismatches singular "nicio" with plural "două" — a native editor would rephrase (e.g. "astfel încât să nu existe două fețe de aceeași culoare care să împărtășească o muchie"). This does not mislead a learner; the concept is fully identifiable.

examples.source: no two faces of the same color share an edge
examples.translation: nicio două fețe de aceeași culoare să nu împărtășească o muchie
examples.suggested: astfel încât să nu existe două fețe de aceeași culoare care să împărtășească o muchie
examples.why: Number-agreement error: singular 'nicio' with plural 'două fețe' is ungrammatical; a native technical writer would rephrase.

editor_note: Faithful, complete, and non-misleading throughout; informal "tu" register holds (Folosește, Începe, Explorează) and core terms are correct and consistent (observabile, valorilor de așteptare, eșantionare, transpiler, porți fracționare, Hamiltonian). Two minor word-choice slips a native technical editor would fix: (1) "candidați promiși" renders "promising candidates" as "promised candidates" — the intended word is "promițători". (2) "greutățile spectrale" translates "spectral weights" with the physical-weight word "greutăți"; the conventional term is "ponderi spectrale". Neither misleads the learner; both are stylistic/terminology polish, not FAIL-grade.

examples.source: candidates for achieving computational advantage
examples.translation: candidați promiși pentru obținerea unui avantaj computațional
examples.suggested: candidați promițători pentru obținerea unui avantaj computațional
examples.why: 'promiși' means 'promised' (past participle of a promite); 'promising' should be 'promițători'. Recognizable but wrong word.

examples.source: cost function values or spectral weights
examples.translation: valorile funcției de cost sau greutățile spectrale
examples.suggested: valorile funcției de cost sau ponderile spectrale
examples.why: 'greutăți' is physical weight/heaviness; the domain term 'spectral weights' is conventionally 'ponderi spectrale' in Romanian physics usage.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, and pedagogically equivalent to the English; core terms correct and consistent (snapshots→instantanee, noise model→model de zgomot, fake backends kept, Clifford circuits→circuite Clifford, "utilitatea lor atinge un plafon la aproximativ 50 de qubiți" for the ~50-qubit ceiling). Informal "tu" register holds throughout (migrezi, specifici, poți).

Meaning is faithful throughout — no semantic drift, no hallucination. Core terms are correct and consistent (Hamiltonian, spațiu Krylov, stare fundamentală, suprapunere polinomială, valori proprii, stare de referință Neel) and the informal "tu" register holds (Reamintește-ți, Verifică-ți, gândește-te, faci). Only minor native-editor slips: a grammatical agreement error "prea puținor pași" (should be "prea puțini pași") at line 71; the adjective left untranslated in "erori Trotterization" (line 71, EN "Trotterization errors"); and a light calque "optimizare peste mulți parametri" (line 293, EN "optimization over many parameters", more natural as "optimizarea multor parametri"). None of these mislead a learner.

examples.source: Using too few steps leads to larger Trotterization errors
examples.translation: Folosirea prea puținor pași duce la erori Trotterization mai mari
examples.suggested: Folosirea prea puținilor pași duce la erori de Trotterizare mai mari
examples.why: 'prea puținor' is a wrong agreement form (should be 'prea puțini'/'prea puținilor'), and 'Trotterization' is left in English where 'de Trotterizare' is natural.

examples.source: it does not require optimization over many parameters in a variational ansatz
examples.translation: nu necesită optimizare peste mulți parametri într-un ansatz variațional
examples.suggested: nu necesită optimizarea multor parametri într-un ansatz variațional
examples.why: 'optimizare peste mulți parametri' is a literal calque of 'optimization over'; Romanian prefers the genitive 'optimizarea multor parametri'. Meaning preserved.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, and pedagogically equivalent to the English; informal "tu" is held throughout (vei înțelege, reține, alegem) and core terms are correct and consistent (adâncimea cu doi qubiți, circuit oglindă, manageri de pași, transpilerul bazat pe AI, outlieri, fidelitate). No semantic drift or hallucination was found — the analysis, mirror-circuit caveats, and recommendations all match the source. The single editorial nit: "a shallower circuit" is rendered as "un circuit mai superficial" (line 857); in Romanian "superficial" reads as "cursory/not thorough" rather than "of low depth", so a native technical writer would prefer "un circuit mai puțin adânc"/"cu adâncime mai mică". This is a calque, not misleading.

examples.source: A compilation method that produces a shallower circuit with fewer gates will accumulate less noise.
examples.translation: O metodă de compilare care produce un circuit mai superficial cu mai puține porți va acumula mai puțin zgomot.
examples.suggested: ...care produce un circuit mai puțin adânc (de adâncime mai mică) cu mai puține porți...
examples.why: "mai superficial" is a calque; in Romanian it connotes "cursory/shallow-in-quality" rather than the circuit-depth sense intended by "shallower". Recognizable but not what a native technical writer would use.

editor_note: Faithful and natural throughout; core terms rendered with correct conventional Romanian (expectation values→"valorile de așteptare", sampling→"eșantionării", standard error→"eroarea standard", error bars→"bare de eroare"), and the "seven observables"/"two-qubit Bell state" quantities and the savefig/show caveats match the source exactly. Informal "tu" register holds consistently (creează, apelează, folosești, poți).

The Romanian is faithful, complete, and reads naturally; meaning and all load-bearing quantities/negations are intact, so the automated FAIL is overturned. The only defects are minor terminology/consistency slips (see examples) that do not mislead a learner.

examples.source: The layers used to entangle many qubits make the circuits very complex / deep
examples.translation: Straturile folosite pentru a încrucișa mulți qubiți fac circuitele foarte complexe/adânci
examples.suggested: Straturile folosite pentru a întrica mulți qubiți fac circuitele foarte complexe/adânci
examples.why: 'a încrucișa' means to cross/interbreed, not quantum entanglement; the conventional Romanian sense is 'a întrica' (întricare). Non-misleading here because the sentence's actual point is circuit depth/noise, but it is the wrong domain term.

examples.source: Require quantum fault tolerance to achieve a result.
examples.translation: Necesită toleranță la defecte cuantice pentru a obține un rezultat.
examples.suggested: Necesită toleranță cuantică la erori pentru a obține un rezultat.
examples.why: 'fault tolerance' is rendered 'toleranței la erori' at line 23 but 'toleranță la defecte' here — same concept, two renderings within the file.

examples.source: This is the ideal use of a simulator.
examples.translation: Acesta este utilizarea ideală a unui simulator.
examples.suggested: Aceasta este utilizarea ideală a unui simulator.
examples.why: Gender-agreement slip: 'utilizarea' is feminine, so the demonstrative should be 'Aceasta', not 'Acesta'.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, and consistent; the Estimator/Sampler contrast is correct (Estimator คำนวณ expectation value ของ observable เทียบกับ state..., Sampler สุ่มตัวอย่างจาก output register), kept-English domain terms (primitives, backend, abstraction, error mitigation) are used consistently, and the backend.run supersession claim on line 38 is preserved accurately. Informal register holds with no polite particles. Only defensible paraphrases (e.g. "would not and should not be familiar with distilling" compressed to "ไม่จำเป็นต้องรู้วิธีแปลงข้อมูลดิบ"), nothing misleading.

Faithful, complete, and correctly informal; no semantic drift or hallucination, and the two criteria for quantum advantage (i/ii) plus the end-of-2026 timeline are all rendered accurately. Only minor calques a native editor would smooth: "next critical frontier" is rendered "ชายแดนที่สำคัญถัดไป" (ชายแดน = a geographic border/frontier, translationese here; พรมแดน/ก้าวสำคัญ would be natural); "post-processing" is rendered "การประมวลผลหลังการทดลอง" twice (lines 42, 44), literally "processing after the experiment," which subtly narrows the sense of generic classical post-processing; and "error suppression" as "การปราบปรามข้อผิดพลาด" (ปราบปราม = crack down/suppress crime) is stiff. All recognizable and non-misleading.

examples.source: the next critical frontier
examples.translation: ชายแดนที่สำคัญถัดไป
examples.suggested: พรมแดนสำคัญถัดไป / ก้าวสำคัญถัดไป
examples.why: ชายแดน is a geographic border; a calque for the abstract 'frontier'. A native technical writer would use พรมแดน or rephrase.

examples.source: classical post-processing
examples.translation: การประมวลผลหลังการทดลองแบบคลาสสิก
examples.suggested: การประมวลผลภายหลังแบบคลาสสิก
examples.why: หลังการทดลอง adds 'after the experiment', slightly narrowing generic post-processing; recognizable but imprecise.

examples.source: hardware-level error suppression
examples.translation: การปราบปรามข้อผิดพลาดระดับฮาร์ดแวร์
examples.suggested: การกดข้อผิดพลาด/การระงับข้อผิดพลาดระดับฮาร์ดแวร์
examples.why: ปราบปราม connotes cracking down on crime; stiff literal choice for technical 'suppression'.

editor_note: Faithful and native throughout; all load-bearing caveats preserved — max_time TTL "ควรตั้งค่าให้มากกว่าเวลา execute ของ job ที่ยาวที่สุด" (line 95), the non-configurable interactive TTL "1 นาทีสำหรับทุกแผน" (line 102), the close-batch flow (line 116), and the deprecation caution "backend=backend เป็น deprecated ตั้งแต่ Qiskit Runtime v0.24.0 แนะนำให้ใช้ parameter mode แทน" (line 243). Domain terms (batch, primitive job, backend, QPU, queue, execute) consistent; informal register holds with no polite particles.

Faithful and natural throughout; the phase-kickback caveat (workspace qubit stays in $\vert-\rangle$, "สามารถนำกลับมาใช้ใหม่...หรือจะละทิ้งก็ได้"), the search-branch logic ($f(x)=1$→พบ solution/หยุด, $f(x)=0$→รันอีกครั้ง/ยอมแพ้), and the $t=O(\sqrt{N})$ "ความน่าจะเป็นสูง" conclusion all match the source with no drift; informal register held (no ครับ/ค่ะ).

Meaning is faithful end-to-end: the caveats ("ไม่ใช่เวลาที่ใช้สำหรับการเรียกเก็บเงิน" for the not-for-billing note, "ใช้ได้เฉพาะกับ Qiskit Runtime Sampler jobs เท่านั้น"), the raw-data field descriptions, and the closing example ("control pulse สำหรับ X gate ที่ใช้กับ qubit 10 ที่ 1161 dt") all match the source with no drift or hallucination; register is consistently informal (no ครับ/ค่ะ). The one slip: EN "a single instruction on some channel" is rendered "คำสั่งเดียวบน channel บางส่วน", where บางส่วน reads as "part of a channel" rather than "some/a certain channel" — a mild calque, not misleading. Not a FAIL.

examples.source: Each string represents a single instruction on some channel and is comma-separated
examples.translation: แต่ละ string แสดงคำสั่งเดียวบน channel บางส่วน และคั่นด้วยเครื่องหมายจุลภาค
examples.suggested: ...คำสั่งเดียวบน channel หนึ่ง...
examples.why: 'channel บางส่วน' reads as 'part of a channel'; the source means 'some/a particular channel'. Minor, non-misleading phrasing slip.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no semantic drift or hallucination. I verified the risk-prone passages: the None-vs-InstructionProperties return logic (line 107: "จะ return ค่า None (หาก instruction รองรับแต่ไม่มี properties เฉพาะถูก assign)"), the "three or more qubits" constraint (line 231: "gates สามหรือมากกว่า qubits"), the v1.x support-window dates (line 255: bug-fix กันยายน 2024 / security มีนาคม 2025), and the "variable delay" caveat for Delay/BoxOp (line 103) all match the English exactly. One real defect: line 41 has a spelling error — "คุณอาจเคียนว่า" should be "คุณอาจเขียนว่า" (the verb "write"). Register is correctly informal (no ครับ/ค่ะ). Prose leans on heavy English code-switching (per the file's keep-English convention), which reads as competent MT rather than native technical prose, but it never misleads.

examples.source: For example, you might have written:
examples.translation: ตัวอย่างเช่น คุณอาจเคียนว่า:
examples.suggested: ตัวอย่างเช่น คุณอาจเขียนว่า:
examples.why: 'เคียน' is a misspelling of 'เขียน' (to write) — a typo a native editor would correct; not misleading but a clear defect.

editor_note: Faithful and natural throughout; the commuting-observables caveat keeps its negation ("จะไม่ถูกประมาณด้วยการวัดเดียวกัน"), broadcasting rules and Example 3/4 quantities (5x1, 1x6, two 3x1→3x6) are correct, and BitArray axis orientation (แกนซ้ายสุด...ordered shots / แกนขวาสุด...bytes) matches the source. Core terms kept consistently in English (expectation values, observables, broadcasting, shots, qubit) with idiomatic Thai framing; informal register holds with no polite particles.

Faithful and natural throughout; the auth caveat is preserved correctly (line 85 "Qiskit MCP Server และ Qiskit Docs MCP Server ไม่ต้องการ authentication" = do NOT require auth), core terms are consistent (transpile→transpile, ควอนตัมคอมพิวติง, quantum circuits/backends kept in English per convention), and the casual register holds with no polite particles.

Faithful and natural throughout; conditions/negations/quantities all preserved — "kung hindi tinukoy, ang default nito ay 10 minuto", "Hindi ito naaangkop sa mga Pay-As-You-Go instance", and "`[]` ibig sabihin walang backends" all match the source, with "Required"→"Kinakailangan" and consistent informal register.

Faithful and non-misleading throughout; all quantitative claims and caveats (100+ physical qubits, $2^{100}$ / $10^{30}$ bits, 30–35 qubit state-vector limit, 3-of-4 NIST methods, up-to-30-years confidentiality, exponential-parallelism disclaimer) are preserved, and the informal register holds (ka/mo/iyong, no po/opo). Three minor slips a native technical editor would tidy: (1) "characterizes" rendered as "Ikinukulay" (literally "colors/tints it"), an odd calque where "Inilalarawan/binibigyang-katangian" is natural; (2) quantum "interfere with each other" rendered "makasagabal sa isa't isa" (to obstruct/get in the way), which carries a negative connotation and loses the interference sense — though "minsan sa kapaki-pakinabang na paraan" keeps the point intact; (3) "nascent academic theories" rendered "naghihintay na academic theory" ("waiting"), which softens "emerging/newborn." None of these teach a false concept, so MINOR, not FAIL.

examples.source: It characterizes the company's progressive capabilities to leverage quantum technology to disrupt industries
examples.translation: Ikinukulay nito ang progresibong kakayahan ng kumpanya na gamitin ang quantum technology para guluhin ang mga industriya
examples.suggested: Inilalarawan/binibigyang-katangian nito ang progresibong kakayahan ng kumpanya...
examples.why: 'Ikinukulay' (literally 'colors/tints') is a calque for 'characterizes'; reads unnatural to a native technical writer, though not misleading.

examples.source: the many quantum states in superposition ... can interfere with each other, sometimes in a useful way
examples.translation: ang maraming quantum state sa superposisyon ay maaaring manipulahin ... at maaaring makasagabal sa isa't isa, minsan sa kapaki-pakinabang na paraan
examples.suggested: ...maaaring mag-interfere sa isa't isa...
examples.why: 'makasagabal' means to obstruct/hinder (negative), losing the neutral/positive quantum 'interference' sense; recognizable but imperfect. Minor.

examples.source: relies on nascent academic theories to generate solutions
examples.translation: umaasa sa mga naghihintay na academic theory para makabuo ng mga solusyon
examples.why: 'nascent' (emerging/newborn) rendered as 'naghihintay' (waiting) slightly softens/shifts the meaning; minor, not misleading.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, and pedagogically clear throughout; no semantic drift or hallucination found. Core terminology is correct and consistent (noise→"ingay" in prose while compounds like noise factor/noise model/noise learning stay English; expectation values and mitigation rendered consistently; e.g. line 914 "malaking paglihis mula sa mga eksaktong halaga" matches EN "high deviation from exact values"). The only defect is a within-file register inconsistency: the file overwhelmingly uses informal singular "mo/iyong/mong" (lines 525, 617, 651), but line 750 switches to the formal plural "maaari na ninyong tingnan ang inyong mga resulta... Kukuha kayo...". This is a MINOR register slip, not misleading.

examples.source: Once the experiment is finished, you can view your results. You fetch the raw and mitigated expectation values...
examples.translation: Kapag natapos na ang eksperimento, maaari na ninyong tingnan ang inyong mga resulta. Kukuha kayo ng raw at mitigated na expectation values...
examples.suggested: Kapag natapos na ang eksperimento, maaari mo nang tingnan ang iyong mga resulta. Kukuha ka ng raw at mitigated na expectation values...
examples.why: Switches to formal plural 'ninyo/inyo/kayo', inconsistent with the informal singular 'mo/iyong' used everywhere else in the file (e.g. lines 525, 617, 651). Register slip, not a meaning change.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no semantic drift, no dropped caveats, correct quantities in the benchmark table, and the final "hindi pa naipapatupad sa kasalukuyang bersyon" caveat is preserved. Core terms (qubit, ensemble, classifier, regularization, VQE/QAOA) are kept in English consistently per field convention. The only defects are grammatical/naturalness slips a native technical editor would fix: the object-focus verb "malutas" is mis-agreed with an ang-marked actor ("maaari kang malutas ng mga tunay na problema" on line 42, should read "maaari mong malutas/lutasin ang mga tunay na problema"). Also "two interleaving half circles" is rendered two different ways — "magkabaluktot" (line 52) vs "magkasalubong" (line 89) — both loose but recognizable renderings of "interleaving". Neither would mislead a learner.

examples.source: you can solve real-world machine learning problems on quantum hardware without requiring quantum expertise.
examples.translation: maaari kang malutas ng mga tunay na problema sa machine learning sa quantum hardware nang hindi kailangan ng kaalaman sa quantum.
examples.suggested: maaari mong lutasin ang mga tunay na problema sa machine learning sa quantum hardware nang hindi kailangan ng kaalaman sa quantum.
examples.why: Object-focus verb 'malutas' is mis-agreed with the ang-marked actor 'kang'; a native writer would use actor-marked 'mo' with the object 'ang mga problema'. Grammatical slip, meaning intact.

examples.translation: dalawang magkabaluktot na kalahating bilog (line 52) vs dalawang magkasalubong na kalahating bilog na hugis-buwan (line 89)

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no semantic drift, inverted conditions, swapped quantities, or hallucinations; the garbage-cleanup and invertible-function sections track the English closely. Three minor slips a native technical editor would change: (1) line 146 renders "which is necessarily unique when it exists" as "kapag umaandar ito" ("when it operates/runs") rather than existence ("kapag umiiral ito") — a small conceptual drift but not misleading in context; (2) line 73 "Hayaan ang $t = \operatorname{size}(C)$ ang bilang..." is a stiff calque of English "Let ... be the number" that a native writer would phrase differently; (3) line 98 "ito ay maikli para sa *basura*" literally calques "it's short for garbage." Also stray English adverbs kept mid-sentence ("essentially" L47, "Partikular" L112). All MINOR — none teach a false concept.

examples.suggested: ...(na kinakailangang natatangi kapag umiiral ito).

examples.translation: ito ay maikli para sa *basura*
examples.why: Literal calque of the idiom 'short for'; the intended sense is 'it stands for/abbreviates garbage'. Understandable but non-native.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, informal-register translation that keeps domain terms in English consistently (qubit, gate, circuit, Bell pair, fidelity, feedforward, entanglement, transpilation) and gets the load-bearing claims right — e.g. the crossover result ("hindi pare-parehong mas mahusay ang LRCX... sa maikling pagkakahiwalay... unitary ay nakamit ng mas mataas na fidelity"), the odd/even branching ("kakaiba ang bilang"/"pantay na distansya"), and the readout-vs-2Q figures (2,280 ns vs 68 ns). Three non-misleading blemishes: (1) the English explanatory paragraph before the IfElseOp code (source ~line 581, "The following step ensures that the backend supports the `if_else` instruction... we explicitly add the `IfElseOp`...") is dropped entirely from the Tagalog — the code stays but its rationale is gone; (2) "non-entangled/unentangled neighboring qubits" is rendered "hindi magkasamang kalapit na qubit" (literally "not-together"), which reads as adjacency rather than the quantum non-entangled state, weakly inconsistent with "naka-entangle" used elsewhere; (3) "not unexpected" comes out as the stilted double-negative "hindi hindi inaasahan." None cause a learner to form a false belief.

examples.source: The following step ensures that the backend supports the `if_else` instruction, which is required for the newer version of dynamic circuits. Since this feature is still in early access, we explicitly add the `IfElseOp` to the backend target if it is not already available.
examples.translation: (omitted — no Tagalog rendering of this paragraph; code block appears with no preceding explanation)
examples.suggested: Tinitiyak ng sumusunod na hakbang na sinusuportahan ng backend ang `if_else` na instruksyon, na kailangan para sa mas bagong bersyon ng dynamic na circuit. Dahil nasa early access pa ang feature na ito, tahasan nating idinaragdag ang `IfElseOp` sa backend target kung wala pa ito.
examples.why: A full pedagogical paragraph explaining why the IfElseOp code exists is dropped, leaving the code unmotivated; omission, not a false statement, so MINOR.

examples.source: non-entangled neighboring qubits / unentangled neighboring qubits
examples.translation: mga hindi magkasamang kalapit na qubit
examples.suggested: mga kalapit na qubit na hindi naka-entangle
examples.why: 'hindi magkasama' reads as 'not together/adjacent' rather than the quantum sense 'not entangled', slightly weakening a domain term and diverging from 'naka-entangle' used elsewhere.

examples.source: This behavior is not unexpected on current hardware
examples.translation: Ang gawi na ito ay hindi hindi inaasahan sa kasalukuyang hardware
examples.why: The double negative 'hindi hindi inaasahan' is stiff and awkward; meaning is preserved but a native writer would say 'inaasahan' or 'hindi nakagugulat'.

editor_note: Faithful and natural; quantities, conditions, and caveats preserved (e.g. line 151 "1.0 = walang approximation, 0.0 = maximum na approximation" matches source directions, line 138 keeps the "Hindi ginagarantiyahan na magiging pareho ang final layout" caveat, and asap/alap scheduling descriptions at lines 157-158 stay correctly distinguished with the default flag on alap). Core domain terms (transpiler, backend, coupling map, qubit, basis gates) kept in English consistently; informal register holds throughout.

Faithful and natural throughout; the "many of the same building blocks... can be applied to a wide range of fields outside quantum chemistry" caveat, the intermediate-level prerequisites ("Hindi kinakailangan ang background sa VQE", 6–10 oras), the exam/badge flow, and the full Credly privacy notice all preserve meaning with no drift or dropped qualifiers. Domain terms kept in English per convention (VQE, ansatz, ground state energies, bond lengths) and register stays informal (matututunan mo, iyong badge).

Faithful and natural throughout; core terms correct and consistent (provider→провайдер, basis gates→базові вентилі, transpile→транспілювати, quantum workloads→квантові робочі навантаження), informal register holds ("Докладніше дивись..." line 50), no semantic drift or dropped caveats in the two prose sections.

Faithful, natural, and pedagogically clear; informal "ти" register consistent and core terminology correct/consistent (гамільтоніан, троттеризація, очікувані значення = expectation values, спостережувані = observables, ансатц, примітив Estimator/Sampler). Two minor slips a native technical editor would fix, neither misleading: (1) line ~291 renders "layer-based noise model" as "послідовної моделі шуму" (послідовна = sequential/consecutive), a small terminology drift from the per-layer sense the PEA context requires — should be "шарової моделі шуму"; (2) line ~110 renders "product state" as "продуктовий стан", a calque that reads oddly in Ukrainian (продуктовий evokes "grocery"); conventional is "стан-добуток"/"добуток станів". Neither would cause a learner to form a false belief.

examples.source: PEA operates under the assumption of a layer-based noise model similar to that used in probabilistic error cancellation (PEC).
examples.translation: PEA працює за припущення послідовної моделі шуму, подібної до тієї, що використовується в ймовірнісному усуненні помилок (PEC).
examples.suggested: ...за припущення шарової моделі шуму...
examples.why: 'послідовної' means sequential/consecutive, not 'layer-based'; PEA characterizes noise per entangling layer, so the shar-based sense should be preserved. Minor drift, not misleading.

examples.source: A common and practical choice is a product state, where qubits are not entangled...
examples.translation: Поширеним та практичним вибором є продуктовий стан, де кубіти не заплутані...
examples.suggested: ...є стан-добуток (добуток станів), де кубіти не заплутані...
examples.why: 'продуктовий стан' is a calque of 'product state' that reads unnaturally (продуктовий = grocery-related); the conventional Ukrainian domain term is 'стан-добуток'/'добуток станів'.

editor_note: Faithful and fluent overall; informal "ти" register is consistent (хочеш, Виконай, Використовуй, за тебе) and core terms are correct/consistent (gate→вентиль, circuit→схема, transpile→транспіляція, QPU/backend/Executor/samplex kept in English). Two minor terminology slips, neither misleading: (1) "25 shots" is rendered "25 вимірювань" (measurements) on line 115 — a recognizable approximation, and the code still shows shots=25; (2) the "Executor broadcasting semantics" link on line 194 is rendered "семантику мовлення Executor", where "мовлення" is the radio/media sense of "broadcasting" rather than the array/shape sense intended, but it is only a nav-link label pointing to the correct page. No semantic inversions, swapped quantities, dropped caveats, or hallucinations in the body prose.

examples.source: specifies to perform 25 shots
examples.translation: вказує виконати 25 вимірювань
examples.suggested: вказує виконати 25 «пострілів» (shots)
examples.why: 'shots' rendered as 'вимірювань' (measurements); a recognizable approximation but not the conventional domain term, and the code block keeps shots=25.

examples.source: Learn about Executor broadcasting semantics.
examples.translation: Дізнайся про семантику мовлення Executor.
examples.suggested: Дізнайся про семантику трансляції (broadcasting) Executor.
examples.why: 'broadcasting' rendered as 'мовлення' (radio/media broadcasting) rather than the array/shape-broadcasting sense; wrong sense, though it is only a nav-link label to the correct page.

editor_note: Faithful, native, and pedagogically equivalent; core terms correct and consistent (gate→вентиль Адамара, ancilla→допоміжний біт, amplitude amplification→підсилення амплітуди, diffusion operator→оператор дифузії, phase kickback→фазовий відкат). Quantities and claims all preserved (e.g. "найнижчою ймовірністю ... зменшилась ще більше" for the 4-iteration result), and informal "ти" register holds throughout ("Зверни увагу", "Імпортуй").

Faithful, natural, and meaning-accurate throughout — no semantic drift or hallucination; informal "ти" register is consistent (Виконай, встанови, викличи, Дізнайся) and core terms are correct (expectation value→очікуване значення, observable→спостережуване, circuit→схема, primitive→примітив, gate→вентиль). Two minor consistency slips a native editor would tidy: (1) "the transpiler" is left as English "Transpiler" at line 98 ("Використовуй Transpiler для цього") while the same concept is Ukrainized elsewhere ("Транспіляція"/"транспілятора" at lines 160–161); (2) "observable" wavers in grammatical gender — neuter "спостережуване" (lines 14, 80) vs masculine "спостережуваний/спостережуваного" (lines 82, 98, 125). Neither misleads the learner.

examples.source: Use the transpiler to do this.
examples.translation: Використовуй Transpiler для цього.
examples.suggested: Використовуй транспілятор для цього.
examples.why: Generic lowercase 'transpiler' is left in English here but Ukrainized as 'транспілятора/Транспіляція' at lines 160–161; unify to the Ukrainian rendering.

examples.source: one or more observables
examples.translation: спостережуване (neuter) vs спостережуваний (masculine)
examples.suggested: Pick one gender for the substantivized 'observable' and use it throughout
examples.why: 'observable' appears as neuter спостережуване (lines 14, 80) and as masculine спостережуваний/спостережуваного (lines 82, 98, 125); a native editor would make the gender consistent.

editor_note: Faithful, natural, and pedagogically clear; core terms consistent (ансатц, функція витрат, градієнт, порожні плато, референсний стан) and informal "ти" register holds (line 215 "ти навчився"). Two minor slips, neither misleading: in image alt-text (line 200) "manifold" is rendered "різновид" (means "variety/kind") where the conventional math term is "многовид"; and a few English qualifiers are softened — "can help our optimizer converge faster" → "допомагає" (helps, line 119), "They may coincide" → "Вони збігаються" (line 119), "which can be very slow" → plain "повільна швидкість збіжності" (line 162). A native technical editor would restore the manifold term and the hedges, but no learner would form a false belief.

examples.source: A complicated curved manifold with many peaks and troughs.
examples.translation: Складний вигнутий різновид з багатьма піками і западинами.
examples.suggested: Складний вигнутий многовид з багатьма піками і западинами.
examples.why: 'manifold' (mathematical surface) is rendered as 'різновид' (variety/kind), a false friend; the domain term is 'многовид'. Confined to image alt-text, so minor.

examples.source: the convergence speed, which can be very slow
examples.translation: повільна швидкість збіжності
examples.why: The 'can be very slow' possibility is flattened to a flat 'slow', dropping the hedge; does not invert meaning.

editor_note: The prose is faithful, fluent, and pedagogically clear throughout; informal "ти" register is held consistently (Зрозумій, ініціалізуй, додай, Дивись), and the tricky output-shape passages (набори параметрів, запуски, біти регістра → (10, 1024, 3); корекціями бітових перекидань для відміни вимірювального twirling → (28, 10, 1, 3)) are rendered accurately with no drift, swapped quantities, or dropped caveats. The one blemish is terminology: in the "Наступні кроки" link list, "Executor broadcasting" is rendered as "мовлення Executor" (line 246). "Мовлення" means broadcasting in the radio/TV/speech sense, not the array/dimension-expansion sense meant here (NumPy-style broadcasting); it is a wrong technical term, but confined to a navigation link label whose target page would clarify, so it is a MINOR slip rather than a misleading concept in the body text.

examples.source: Understand [Executor broadcasting]
examples.translation: Зрозумій [мовлення Executor]
examples.suggested: Зрозумій [трансляцію (broadcasting) Executor]
examples.why: 'Мовлення' means radio/TV broadcasting or speech, not the array-dimension broadcasting concept the guide refers to; the correct sense is dimensional 'трансляція' or keeping 'broadcasting' in English. Confined to a Next-steps link label, so MINOR.

editor_note: Faithful, native, and pedagogically clear; core terms correct and consistent (superposition→суперпозиція, entanglement→заплутаність, interference→інтерференція, qubit→кубіт, gate→вентиль, circuit→схема), collapse rendered idiomatically as «згортається до одного чистого стану», and the informal "ти" register holds throughout (переглянь, розглянь, пам'ятай). Semantic-heavy passages in the last 40% (scarf entanglement metaphor, classical-vs-quantum probability, key takeaways incl. «слабша за експоненційний паралелізм») all preserve meaning and caveats. Only a mild paraphrase at line 41 («стали означати різні типи обчислень» for "came to modify the word 'computing'") slightly flattens the grammar-analogy point, but it is defensible and non-misleading.
